text.title: Win-Loss Quarterly PM Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Win-Loss Quarterly PM Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Synthesis stitches three sources: (a) CRM data (lost-reason distribution), (b) interview themes, (c) competitive intel. Mismatch surfaces blind spots.
rule.rationale: Single-source synthesis has known biases; three-source triangulation is the validity check.

rule.statement: Quarterly cadence with fixed dates (e.g., 2 weeks after quarter close). Annual = stale; monthly = noise.
rule.rationale: Quarterly is the natural cadence for B2B sales cycles; explicit slot reduces drift.

rule.statement: Output: top 3 win themes + top 3 loss themes, each with ≥3 evidence sources (CRM/interview/competitor). >3 themes dilute action.
rule.rationale: Forced top-3 prevents 'everything matters' synthesis; 3 themes = actionable scope.

rule.statement: Per theme, ≥1 decision: product (roadmap item), messaging (marketing copy update), sales (objection-handling). No theme is a 'note for next time'.
rule.rationale: Themes without decisions = inert research; explicit decisions tie research to action.

rule.statement: Synthesis distributed within 14 days of quarter close. Follow-up at next quarter: which decisions shipped?
rule.rationale: Without distribution + follow-up, synthesis is filed; loop closes only with follow-up.
